resposta:
solution: 
```
class Solution:
    def removeZeroSumSublists(self, head: Optional[ListNode]) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        dummy = ListNode(0)
        dummy.next = head
        curr_sum = 0
        node = dummy
        while node:
            curr_sum += node.val
            node = node.next
            while curr_sum < 0:
                curr_sum -= node.val
                node = node.next
            if curr_sum == 0:
                node = dummy
                while node.next != node:
                    node = node.next
                node.next = node.next.next
                return dummy.next
        return head
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(N)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a two-pointer approach to efficiently remove the zero-sum sublists. It maintains a running sum of the node values and moves the pointers accordingly. When the sum becomes zero, it removes the sublist by updating the dummy node's next pointer. The time complexity is O(N) as each node is visited once, and the space complexity is O(1) as only a constant amount of space is used.
